response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack engineer with solid practices in TDD, event-driven architecture, and high-throughput systems, but his background is almost entirely web and TypeScript/Node.js. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a student thesis project — far short of the senior-level C++/STL depth this role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation experience, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or secure development lifecycle practices aligned with IEC 62443, which are central to the position.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O)
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile — aligns with the role's quality and collaboration expectations
Hands-on exposure to event-driven and message-broker architectures through GISCollective and Dunnhumby work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — approximately 6 months at NI (2014–2015) plus a university thesis; nowhere near the senior architect-level C++/STL/multi-threading depth required
No industrial automation, OT/ICS, or IoT background; no mention of MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, or any industrial protocol
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, which are explicitly required and non-negotiable for this role
